active_paradigms.description: Replace SLSQP with differential_evolution, a stochastic global optimizer that doesn't require gradient information and is better at escaping local optima. Use the 'best1bin' strategy with population size 15-20. Encode variables as [x0,y0,r0,x1,y1,r1,...] with bounds [(r,1-r), (r,1-r), (0.05,0.2)] for each circle. Implement constraints using the penalty method: add large penalty (e.g., 1000) for each constraint violation to the objective. Run with polish=True to refine the final solution using L-BFGS-B.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum of radii - global optimizer can find better solutions than local SLSQP
active_paradigms.cautions: Set maxiter=1000, tol=1e-7, seed=42 for reproducibility. Penalty function must be smooth. Population size affects runtime - balance quality vs speed.

active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with trust-constr method and analytic Jacobian/Hessian
active_paradigms.description: Switch from SLSQP to trust-constr which handles nonlinear constraints more robustly. Provide analytic Jacobians for both objective and constraints to improve convergence. The objective Jacobian is simple (partial derivatives of -sum(r) w.r.t. each variable). For constraints, compute analytic gradients of distance functions. Use sparse constraint Jacobian matrix for efficiency. Initialize from multiple diverse starting points including asymmetric patterns, not just hexagonal.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum of radii - better constraint handling and faster convergence with analytic derivatives
active_paradigms.cautions: trust-constr requires constraints in specific format (NonlinearConstraint). Use sparse matrices for efficiency. Set gtol=1e-9, xtol=1e-9, maxiter=1000.

active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.spatial.Voronoi to guide iterative radius expansion
active_paradigms.description: Start with small circles placed in a hexagonal pattern. Iteratively: (1) Compute Voronoi diagram of all circle centers, (2) For each circle, find the maximum radius it can expand to within its Voronoi cell (bounded by unit square and neighboring circles), (3) Expand each circle to its maximum radius, (4) Reposition centers toward Voronoi cell centroids for better packing. Repeat until convergence. This geometric approach naturally respects constraints and finds dense local packings.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum of radii through geometric space-filling guided by Voronoi tessellation
active_paradigms.cautions: Handle edge cases where Voronoi cells extend outside unit square. Clip cells to square boundaries. Use scipy.spatial.Voronoi with qhull_options='Qbb Qc Qz' for robustness. Limit iterations to prevent infinite loops.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with SLSQP to jointly optimize all 78 variables (26 centers × 2 + 26 radii) with explicit nonlinear constraints
tried_paradigms.description: Flatten all circle parameters into a single vector [x0,y0,r0,x1,y1,r1,...]. Define constraint functions: (1) circle-wall constraints: r_i ≤ x_i ≤ 1-r_i and r_i ≤ y_i ≤ 1-r_i, (2) non-overlap constraints: ||c_i - c_j|| ≥ r_i + r_j for all pairs i<j. Use SLSQP method which handles nonlinear constraints. Start from the current hexagonal pattern as initial guess. The optimizer will adjust both positions and radii simultaneously to maximize sum while respecting all geometric constraints.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Maximize sum of radii by jointly optimizing positions and radii under geometric constraints
tried_paradigms.cautions: SLSQP requires constraints as callable functions returning values ≤ 0 for feasible points. Use multiple restarts from perturbed initial positions. Set ftol and maxiter appropriately. The non-overlap constraints are non-convex so multiple initializations help avoid local minima.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use Voronoi diagram (scipy.spatial.Voronoi) to compute maximum inscribed circle at each site, iteratively refining positions toward Voronoi centers
tried_paradigms.description: Initialize circle centers, then iterate: (1) Compute Voronoi diagram of current centers, (2) For each cell, the maximum inscribed circle radius equals the minimum distance from site to any Voronoi edge or square boundary, (3) Move each center slightly toward its Voronoi cell centroid to improve packing, (4) Repeat until convergence. This geometric approach naturally maximizes radii by pushing circles apart. The Voronoi edges define the 'natural' neighbors for each circle.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Geometric positioning that maximizes each circle's local available space through Voronoi-based analysis
tried_paradigms.cautions: Handle Voronoi cells that extend outside the unit square by clipping to boundaries. Some cells may be unbounded - clip to square. The iteration should use small step sizes to avoid oscillation. This is a fixed-point iteration, not gradient-based optimization.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use force-directed simulation with adaptive radii - model circles as repelling particles that grow until blocked
tried_paradigms.description: Treat circles as physical particles with: (1) Repulsive forces between overlapping circles proportional to overlap distance, (2) Repulsive forces from walls when circle extends outside, (3) Each circle has a 'growth pressure' that tries to increase its radius. Simulate using scipy.integrate.odeint or simple Euler stepping. At each step, grow all radii by small amount, resolve overlaps through position adjustment, repeat. This physics-based approach naturally finds dense packings by letting circles expand until equilibrium.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Equilibrium configuration where all circles are maximally grown and no overlaps exist
tried_paradigms.cautions: Use damping to prevent oscillations. The growth rate should be slow relative to position adjustment. Stop when total growth per iteration falls below threshold. This is a simulation approach, not direct optimization - may need careful tuning of force constants and damping.

tried_paradigms.description: Replace SLSQP with trust-constr, which uses a trust-region approach that handles nonlinear constraints more robustly. The trust-constr method maintains a trust region around the current point and solves a constrained subproblem at each iteration. It's particularly effective for problems with many nonlinear constraints like circle packing. Use the same constraint formulation but increase maxiter to 1000 and use 'lsmr' for the linear system solver. The method better handles the complex constraint landscape of circle packing.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Primary sum_radii metric by finding better local optima
tried_paradigms.cautions: trust-constr can be slower; increase ftol to 1e-10 for precision; use sparse Jacobian for efficiency; may need SequentialBounds modifier for better bound handling

tried_paradigms.description: Differential evolution is a population-based global optimizer that can escape local optima. Modern scipy supports constraints directly via the constraints parameter. Use strategy='best1bin' with popsize=20-30, mutation=(0.5, 1.0), recombination=0.7. The population explores multiple arrangements simultaneously, discovering configurations local methods miss. Set maxiter=1000 and polish=True for final refinement. The constraints parameter accepts NonlinearConstraint objects for the wall and non-overlap constraints.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Global exploration to find better arrangements beyond local optima
tried_paradigms.cautions: Must use scipy >= 1.4 for constraint support; define constraints as NonlinearConstraint with sparse Jacobian; bounds are handled directly; higher popsize increases chance of finding global optimum but costs more time

tried_paradigms.description: SHGO is specifically designed for constrained global optimization. It uses simplicial homology to partition the search space and identify all local minima. For circle packing with many constraints, SHGO can systematically explore the feasible region. Use the 'sobol' sampling method with n=100-200 samples, iters=3-5, and set constraint handling via the constraints parameter. SHGO's mathematical foundation ensures it finds all basins of attraction, making it ideal for highly multimodal problems like circle packing.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Systematic global search to find all local minima and select the best
tried_paradigms.cautions: Computationally intensive; use sampling_method='sobol' for efficiency; may need to reduce n for timeout; returns all local minima so we can select the best; ensure constraints are properly vectorized
